title: Dog Yellow Pus Draining from Ear: Causes and Next Steps
meta_desc: Is yellow pus draining from your dog's ear? Learn about ear infections, urgency levels, and why photos help your vet provide the best care.
content_html:
What is Yellow Pus in Your Dog's Ear?

Yellow discharge, or pus, typically indicates a bacterial infection within the ear canal. This condition, known as otitis externa, occurs when bacteria multiply due to moisture, allergies, or debris trapped in the ear. It is often accompanied by a strong, unpleasant odor, redness, and visible discomfort for your pet.

Is This an Emergency?

The urgency level for yellow pus in the ear is Medium. While usually not a life-threatening emergency in the immediate hour, ear infections are incredibly painful and can lead to a ruptured eardrum or a deeper middle-ear infection if left untreated. You should schedule a veterinary appointment within 24 to 48 hours. If your dog is tilting its head, circling, or seems disoriented, seek care as soon as possible as these can be signs of vestibular issues.

Why a Photo Helps Triage

Providing a clear, well-lit photo of the ear opening and the discharge allows a veterinary professional to assess the severity and type of exudate. Capturing the specific color, consistency, and any swelling helps the triage team determine if the situation requires an urgent care visit or if it can wait for a standard appointment. It also serves as a visual baseline to monitor if the condition worsens while you wait for your visit.

faqs.question: Should I clean my dog's ear before seeing the vet?
faqs.answer: It is usually best to wait. Cleaning the ear can remove the samples your vet needs for cytology (testing) and may accidentally push bacteria or debris deeper into the ear canal if not done correctly.

faqs.question: Can I use human ear drops for my dog's infection?
faqs.answer: No, you should never use human medications or over-the-counter drops without a veterinarian's approval. Some ingredients can be toxic to dogs or cause permanent hearing loss if the eardrum is already ruptured.

faqs.question: What are common signs of ear pain in dogs?
faqs.answer: Common signs include frequent head shaking, scratching at the ear, rubbing the side of the head on the floor, or yelping when the ear or head is touched.
